_meta.review_warning: AI-drafted v0. DO NOT USE IN PRODUCTION until reviewed by a qualified Vic WHS practitioner and approved. Silica controls per AS/NZS 4849.1 + Safe Work Australia silica COP — wet cutting is mandatory.

activity_name: Pool tiling, coping and waterline (cutting + setting)

hazards.title: Respirable crystalline silica — wet/dry cutting tile and stone
hazards.risk: Silicosis from prolonged dust exposure; many imported tiles + engineered stones have very high silica content

controls.title: Specify lower-silica materials at design where feasible
Wet-cut saw MANDATORY for all tile, coping, and stone cutting
Dedicated cutting area away from other workers; bunded for slurry

hazards.title: Tile adhesive / grout — alkaline burns
hazards.risk: Cement-based adhesive and grout — same alkali burn risk as concrete

hazards.title: Cuts from tile / stone shards
hazards.risk: Sharp tile edges; cuts during handling and clean-up

hazards.title: Slips on wet pool floor / coping during set
hazards.risk: Wet pool floor and coping surfaces during tile / coping work

controls.title: Keep wet area to active work zone; squeegee excess

hazards.title: Falls into empty pool
hazards.risk: Pool empty during tile / coping; fall risk into shell

controls.title: Perimeter barrier around pool while empty; no access for non-workers

tasks: Pre-start: shell signed off; coping detail confirmed; tile lay-out / setout drawings on site.
Designate cutting area away from other workers; bunding for slurry water.
Prep substrate per manufacturer (clean, prime as required).
Set out and lay tiles in working courses; check level frequently.
Cutting only on wet-cut saw in designated area; P3 respirator mandatory.
Coping: bed, set, check level / consistent overhang per detail.
Allow appropriate cure time before grouting.
Grout to spec; sponge clean progressively.
Cure water + slurry captured to skip / bund; not into garden or drain.
Cleaning and protective covering ready for handover.
